Title: Genome-Wide Identification of the HD-ZIP III Subfamily in Upland Cotton Reveals the Involvement of GhHB8-5D in the Biosynthesis of Secondary Wall in Fiber and Drought Resistance

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GhHB8-5D is one of two homoeologous genes (GhHB8-5A and GhHB8-5D) forming GhHB8-5, and shows D-subgenome–biased, fiber-preferential expression, likely due to promoter cis-element differences and distinct miR166-mediated cleavage efficiency. A GhHB8-5Dm construct was generated by introducing synonymous mutations at the miR166-binding site in the START domain without altering the protein sequence, making it miRNA-resistant; overexpression of GhHB8-5Dm in Arabidopsis caused distinct changes in vascular organization (amphivasal bundles) and repression of many secondary wall-related genes compared with GhHB8-5D, helping to dissect miRNA-dependent regulation. Functional conclusions for GhHB8-5D are based on heterologous expression in Arabidopsis and not yet on direct genetic manipulation in cotton plants.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. GhHB8-5D is an HD-ZIP III transcription factor from Gossypium hirsutum with HD, LZ, START, SAD, and MEKHLA domains, localizes to the nucleus, and has a MEKHLA-dependent transcriptional activation activity.
2. GhHB8-5D shows fiber-preferential, developmentally increasing expression in cotton and contains drought-related cis-elements in its promoter, suggesting a role in fiber secondary wall thickening and stress responses.
3. Ectopic overexpression of GhHB8-5D in Arabidopsis causes dwarfism, curly leaves, twisted stems, ectopic secondary wall deposition, irregular xylem vessels, and altered vascular patterning, accompanied by upregulation of secondary wall-related transcription factors, cellulose synthase, and lignin biosynthetic genes.
4. Overexpression of GhHB8-5D or a miR166-resistant synonymous mutant GhHB8-5Dm enhances green seedling rate but reduces root length under mannitol-induced drought stress, and perturbs Arabidopsis HD-ZIP III gene expression, indicating that GhHB8-5D positively regulates secondary wall biosynthesis and contributes to drought resistance by affecting xylem morphology and cell wall thickening.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic overexpression of GhHB8-5D in Arabidopsis promotes xylem differentiation and ectopic secondary wall (lignin and cellulose) deposition and enhances tolerance to mannitol-simulated drought stress, indicating that GhHB8-5D acts as a positive regulator of secondary wall thickening associated with drought response.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GhHB8-5D encodes a nuclear transcriptional activator; yeast assays with full-length and C-terminal truncations demonstrate that the transcriptional activation domain resides in the MEKHLA domain. A conserved miR166-binding site in the START domain mediates post-transcriptional regulation; synonymous mutation of this site (GhHB8-5Dm) abolishes miR166 targeting without changing the protein, altering downstream gene expression patterns. Overexpression of GhHB8-5D in Arabidopsis upregulates master regulators of secondary wall formation (AtNST1, AtVND7, AtMYB46), cellulose synthase genes (AtCESA4, AtCESA8), and lignin biosynthetic genes (At4CL1, AtCCoAOMT1, AtCCR1), whereas GhHB8-5Dm overexpression generally represses these genes. GhHB8-5D overexpression also downregulates AtHB8 and AtPHV and upregulates AtREV, while GhHB8-5Dm induces AtPHB and AtPHV and represses AtHB8, AtCNA, and AtREV, indicating that GhHB8-5D perturbs the HD-ZIP III regulatory network controlling xylem differentiation and secondary wall deposition.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions within the secondary cell wall transcriptional network that governs xylem differentiation and lignin/cellulose biosynthesis, influencing downstream NAC/MYB-type master switches, cellulose synthase, and lignin biosynthetic genes; contributes to drought stress response pathways by modifying xylem morphology and secondary wall thickening, which are linked to improved drought resistance.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Secondary cell wall biosynthesis involving cellulose and lignin deposition in vascular tissues and fiber
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: GhHB8-5D overexpression in Arabidopsis activates the expression of secondary wall-related regulators and biosynthetic genes (GhHB8-5D -->(activation)--> AtNST1, AtVND7, AtMYB46, AtCESA4, AtCESA8, At4CL1, AtCCoAOMT1, AtCCR1) and represses some HD-ZIP III members (GhHB8-5D --(repression)--> AtHB8, AtPHV) while activating AtREV (GhHB8-5D -->(activation)--> AtREV). GhHB8-5Dm overexpression represses multiple secondary wall-related genes (GhHB8-5Dm --(repression)--> AtNST1, AtVND7, AtMYB46, AtCESA4, AtCESA7, AtCESA8, At4CL1, AtCCoAOMT1, AtCCR1) and alters HD-ZIP III expression (GhHB8-5Dm -->(activation)--> AtPHB, AtPHV; GhHB8-5Dm --(repression)--> AtHB8, AtCNA, AtREV).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HD-ZIP III transcription factor containing a homeodomain (HD) and leucine zipper (LZ), a START lipid-binding domain, an adjacent SAD region, and a C-terminal MEKHLA domain; the MEKHLA domain harbors the transcriptional activation region.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, shown by GhHB8-5D–eGFP fusion transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf epidermal cells and observed as nuclear GFP fluorescence by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: GhHB8-5D is predominantly expressed in cotton fibers, with transcript levels increasing as fiber development progresses (20–25 DPA); its homoeolog GhHB8-5A shows broader expression in root, flower, and 20–25 DPA fiber. The GhHB8-5D promoter contains MBS cis-elements associated with drought inducibility.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mannitol-induced osmotic stress (50–200 mM) applied on MS medium to mimic drought during Arabidopsis seed germination and seedling growth; cotton fiber developmental stages (0–25 DPA); promoter contains an MBS cis-element related to drought inducibility.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Secondary cell wall formation and fiber development; xylem differentiation; abiotic stress and drought resistance in cotton.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance) and secondary cell wall thickening/xylem differentiation affecting fiber development and vascular morphology.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 100 mM mannitol, the green seedling rate of wild-type Arabidopsis was 71.1%, whereas GhHB8-5D and GhHB8-5Dm overexpression lines reached approximately 84.4–87.8%; at 150 and 200 mM mannitol, all four transgenic lines showed significantly higher green seedling rates than wild type. Without mannitol, primary root lengths of transgenic and wild type seedlings did not differ significantly, but at 50 mM mannitol GhHB8-5Dm lines had significantly shorter roots than wild type, and at 100–200 mM mannitol the primary roots of all transgenic lines were markedly shorter than those of wild type.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In Arabidopsis, GhHB8-5D and GhHB8-5Dm overexpression caused strong growth defects including smaller 3-week-old seedlings, curly leaves, reduced plant height, and twisted inflorescence stems; stem anatomy showed ectopic secondary wall deposition in parenchyma, irregular xylem vessels, and in GhHB8-5Dm lines amphivasal vascular bundles and loss of normal interfascicular fiber secondary walls; under mannitol treatment, transgenic lines displayed shorter primary roots than wild type.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Gossypium hirsutum cv. ZM24 plants; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 transgenic lines overexpressing GhHB8-5D or GhHB8-5Dm; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient GhHB8-5D–eGFP expression; Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain AH109 for transcriptional activation assay; Arabidopsis seedlings grown on MS medium with mannitol.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification and domain analysis of HD-ZIP III genes using BLAST, Pfam, HMMER, CD-Search, MEME; qRT-PCR expression profiling in multiple cotton tissues and developmental stages; PCR amplification and cloning of GhHB8-5D cDNA into pBI121 for 35S-driven overexpression; construction of miR166-resistant synonymous mutant GhHB8-5Dm; generation of stable Arabidopsis transgenic lines; stem sectioning and toluidine blue, phloroglucinol, S4B staining and lignin autofluorescence microscopy; confocal imaging of GhHB8-5D–eGFP in N. benthamiana; yeast one-hybrid–type transcriptional activation assay with X-gal; mannitol treatments to assay green seedling rate and primary root length.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S-driven overexpression of GhHB8-5D and its miR166-binding-site synonymous mutant GhHB8-5Dm in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0; histological and histochemical staining (toluidine blue, phloroglucinol, S4B, lignin autofluorescence) of stem cross sections; qRT-PCR analysis of secondary wall-related genes and Arabidopsis HD-ZIP III genes; mannitol-based drought tolerance and root growth assays; transient GhHB8-5D–eGFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast GAL4 DNA-binding domain fusion transcriptional activation assay in Saccharomyces cerevisiae AH109.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: HD-ZIP III genes including GhHB8-5D were identified from the Gossypium hirsutum (TM-1) genome by BLAST searches using Arabidopsis HD-ZIP III proteins and verification of conserved HD, START, and MEKHLA domains via Pfam, HMMER, and CD-Search; the full coding sequence of GhHB8-5D was PCR-amplified from cotton and cloned into pBI121 under the CaMV 35S promoter for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GhHB8-5D is a promising target for improving cotton fiber quality and drought resistance; manipulating GhHB8-5D expression or miR166-insensitive variants (GhHB8-5Dm-like) could enhance secondary wall thickening and lignification and thereby increase mechanical strength and drought adaptation in cotton.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GhHB8-5Dm differs from GhHB8-5D only by synonymous mutations at the conserved miR166-binding site in the START domain, rendering it miRNA-insensitive; this allows dissection of miR166-mediated regulation of HD-ZIP III activity. GhHB8-5Dm overexpression leads to amphivasal vascular bundles and loss of normal interfascicular secondary wall deposition, likely via induction of AtPHB and AtPHV and repression of AtHB8, AtCNA, and AtREV, demonstrating that miRNA-resistant HD-ZIP III activity can rewire the vascular HD-ZIP III network.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. GhHB8-5Dm is a synonymous, miR166-resistant variant of GhHB8-5D that abolishes miRNA targeting without altering the protein.
2. Overexpression of GhHB8-5Dm in Arabidopsis leads to severe alterations in vascular organization, including amphivasal vascular bundles and loss of normal interfascicular secondary wall deposition.
3. GhHB8-5Dm overexpression generally represses secondary wall-related TFs, cellulose synthase, and lignin biosynthetic genes and strongly perturbs the expression of Arabidopsis HD-ZIP III genes (inducing AtPHB/AtPHV and repressing AtHB8/AtCNA/AtREV).
4. GhHB8-5Dm enhances green seedling rate but causes shorter roots under mannitol-induced drought stress compared with wild type, highlighting the functional importance of miR166 regulation of GhHB8-5D in balancing secondary wall formation and drought responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of the miR166-resistant GhHB8-5Dm in Arabidopsis alters vascular patterning (amphivasal bundles, disrupted interfascicular secondary walls) and generally represses secondary wall-related genes while still enhancing green seedling rate and increasing sensitivity of root growth to mannitol, showing that miRNA-regulated GhHB8-5D activity modulates secondary wall deposition and drought response.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: By removing miR166-mediated repression while preserving GhHB8-5D protein sequence, GhHB8-5Dm shifts the balance of HD-ZIP III activity: overexpression broadly represses secondary wall regulators and biosynthetic genes and alters HD-ZIP III expression (upregulating AtPHB and AtPHV, downregulating AtHB8, AtCNA, AtREV), resulting in disrupted vascular patterning and secondary wall deposition; this demonstrates the importance of miRNA control in tuning HD-ZIP III-dependent secondary wall and drought responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts within the HD-ZIP III-centered secondary wall and vascular development network, where altered miR166 sensitivity changes the regulatory balance among HD-ZIP III members and their downstream secondary wall genes, thereby impacting xylem patterning and drought-related traits.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Secondary cell wall biosynthesis and regulation of lignin and cellulose deposition via altered HD-ZIP III network activity
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: GhHB8-5Dm --(repression)--> AtNST1, AtVND7, AtMYB46, AtCESA4, AtCESA7, AtCESA8, At4CL1, AtCCoAOMT1, AtCCR1; GhHB8-5Dm -->(activation)--> AtPHB, AtPHV; GhHB8-5Dm --(repression)--> AtHB8, AtCNA, AtREV, demonstrating that miRNA-resistant GhHB8-5D activity reshapes the HD-ZIP III and secondary wall regulatory network.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Encodes the same HD-ZIP III protein as GhHB8-5D, containing HD, LZ, START, SAD, and MEKHLA domains; only the miR166-binding site at the nucleotide level is altered by synonymous mutations.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Shares the nuclear localization pattern of GhHB8-5D, as the encoded protein sequence is unchanged; localization was inferred from GhHB8-5D–eGFP experiments.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: GhHB8-5Dm uses the same GhHB8-5D coding sequence at the protein level and therefore reflects the fiber-preferential, developmentally increasing expression pattern of GhHB8-5D in cotton, but the synonymous changes abolish miR166 targeting, effectively altering post-transcriptional regulation rather than spatial expression domains.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mannitol (50–200 mM) applied to transgenic and wild-type Arabidopsis seedlings to simulate drought stress during germination and early root growth.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Engineering miRNA-resistant HD-ZIP III alleles to probe and potentially manipulate secondary cell wall formation, vascular development, and drought resistance in cotton.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance) and secondary cell wall/xylem patterning modulated by altered miRNA regulation of HD-ZIP III activity.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Engineered miR166-resistant GhHB8-5Dm allele that modifies downstream secondary wall and HD-ZIP III gene expression and improves drought-related green seedling performance in Arabidopsis compared with wild type.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 100 mM mannitol, GhHB8-5Dm overexpression lines (L3, L9) show green seedling rates elevated to about 84.4–87.8% compared with 71.1% in wild type; at 50 mM mannitol, their green seedling rate is significantly higher than wild type, and their primary roots are significantly shorter than wild type at 50–200 mM mannitol, indicating increased drought-associated germination performance but stronger inhibition of root elongation.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In Arabidopsis, GhHB8-5Dm overexpression causes twisted inflorescence stems, curly leaves, shortened plant height, aberrant amphivasal vascular bundles, absence of secondary wall deposition at normal interfascicular fiber positions, and occasional ectopic secondary wall deposition in epidermis and phloem; under mannitol stress, GhHB8-5Dm lines exhibit shorter primary roots than wild type.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Gossypium hirsutum cv. ZM24 as the source of GhHB8-5D cDNA used to generate the synonymous miR166-resistant mutant GhHB8-5Dm; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 transgenic lines overexpressing GhHB8-5Dm; Arabidopsis seedlings on mannitol-containing MS medium for drought assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Fusion PCR to introduce synonymous mutations at the miR166-binding site in the GhHB8-5D START domain; cloning of GhHB8-5Dm cDNA into pBI121 under the 35S promoter; generation and selection of homozygous GhHB8-5Dm overexpression Arabidopsis lines; stem sectioning and staining (toluidine blue, phloroglucinol, S4B, lignin autofluorescence); qRT-PCR profiling of secondary wall-related and HD-ZIP III genes; mannitol treatments for green seedling rate and primary root length measurements.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S-driven overexpression of GhHB8-5Dm in Arabidopsis thaliana; histological and histochemical staining of stem cross sections; qRT-PCR of secondary wall-related and HD-ZIP III genes; mannitol-based drought tolerance and root length assays; comparison with GhHB8-5D overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GhHB8-5Dm was generated by fusion PCR introducing synonymous mutations into the miR166-binding site of GhHB8-5D cDNA, using overlapping primers that mismatch the miRNA-binding sequence without altering the encoded protein, followed by cloning into pBI121 for overexpression.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: The miRNA-resistant GhHB8-5Dm variant illustrates that modulating miR166 sensitivity of GhHB8-5D can strongly influence secondary wall-related gene expression, vascular patterning, and drought-associated traits, suggesting that engineered alleles with altered miRNA binding could be exploited to fine-tune fiber secondary wall formation and drought adaptation in cotton breeding or biotechnology.
